clinical.input_text: Clinical demo: back pain with radicular spasm plus saddle anesthesia and new bladder dysfunction.
constraints: Hard constraint: cauda equina red flags cannot be set aside as benign spasm.
Must-not-miss hazard: neurologic compromise with bladder/saddle symptoms.
Blue optimization may only run after red flags are escalated or explicitly ruled out.

red_channel.rationale: RED runs first because neurologic red flags dominate action threshold under uncertainty.

still.name: STILL

checkpoints.name: STILL Checkpoint 1
checkpoints.reason: High-consequence RED hazard remains active; BLUE cannot downgrade escalation.

checkpoints.reason: Red-flag contradiction and denominator collapse require retessellation.

commitment_readiness.rationale: Hypothesis space is undercomplete; rebuild before commitment.

learning_notes: Benign narrative cannot close while red-flag discriminators remain unresolved.
High-consequence uncertainty keeps RED authority active through BLUE reasoning.

audit_events.summary: Checked permission to enter BLUE without clearing RED.

hypotheses.label: Mechanical/radicular spasm
hypotheses.action_threshold: treat only after red flags addressed

support.description: Evidence or plausibility support only; not action priority.

action_priority.description: RED/threshold action class only; not a likelihood magnitude.

contradictions.observation: benign spasm narrative competes with red flags
contradictions.conflicts_with: RED escalation threshold crossed by consequence

density_basis.runtime_gate_note: Frozen MVP demo score only; runtime governance derives contradiction_density from evaluated constraint violations.

voronoi_commitment.recommended_action: Escalate red-flag pathway; do not finalize as benign spasm yet.
voronoi_commitment.consequence_weighting: Missed neurologic emergency dominates unnecessary escalation cost.

non_quiescence.reason: Benign spasm manifold cannot absorb bladder/saddle red flags without contradiction.
non_quiescence.next_required_move: Gather discriminators or escalate; do not calmly finalize.

zeroback.reason: Reset from benign-pain manifold to must-not-miss neurologic emergency frame.
zeroback.reset_scope: Keep symptoms and red flags; reset priors and rebuild hypotheses.

BLUE optimization cannot downgrade unresolved RED hazard
current_state.current_commitment: Escalate red-flag pathway; do not finalize as benign spasm yet.

expected_changes: discriminating data stabilizes benign frame only if red flags are resolved
RED remains active while bladder/saddle findings are unresolved
expected_resolution_signs: benign closure occurs only after RED hazard is ruled out

loop_decision.rationale: Retessellation completed inside the MVP packet; the remaining obligation is next-cycle discriminator verification.
loop_decision.next_observation_required: Obtain and compare red-flag discriminators against the expected trajectory.

audit_events: Declared expected next-state trajectory for high-consequence red-flag uncertainty.
Defined failure conditions that would force hold, retessellation, or ZeroBack.

audit_trace.summary: Parsed pain narrative and red-flag observations.
Screened must-not-miss neurologic hazards before BLUE.
Preserved RED escalation before bounded BLUE analysis.
Compared benign and catastrophic hypotheses inside RED boundary.
Classified benign closure vs red flags as action-threshold contradiction.
Detected omitted emergency class if only benign spasm is considered.
Marked wrong-manifold risk from benign closure.
Set commitment readiness to retessellate.
Rebuilt hypothesis space to include compressive neurologic emergency.
Reset from benign-pain manifold to must-not-miss emergency frame.
Committed to escalation/hold based on consequence-weighted threshold.
Declared next-state observations and failure conditions for the active frame.

final_output.concise_recommendation: Do not close as benign spasm. Escalate or obtain the red-flag discriminators now.
caveats: Deterministic MVP scaffold; not medical advice or a diagnosis.

demo_limitations.limitation: The clinical MVP packet is a deterministic red-flag governance scaffold, not a clinical diagnostic runtime.
demo_limitations.implication: It demonstrates escalation preservation and pending discriminator obligations without observing a live clinical next state.

jailing.input_text: Canonical Jailing/Jingall case: source constraint says the required name is JINGALL, but the candidate answer collapses to the familiar word JAILING.
observations: candidate looks like a plausible common word
constraints: Hard constraint: preserve the source token JINGALL exactly.
Hard constraint: do not normalize a proper token into a plausible nearby word.
Must-not-miss hazard: cosmetic fluency cannot override source identity.

active_hazards.constraint: source token must be preserved exactly

missing_discriminators: Confirm source spelling from the prompt or authoritative record.
Compare candidate token character-by-character against the governed token.
red_channel.rationale: RED runs first because source-token preservation is a hard constraint, not a style preference.

still.name: STILL

checkpoints.name: STILL Checkpoint 1
checkpoints.reason: Source-token constraint risk remains active; BLUE may explain but cannot clear RED.

checkpoints.name: STILL Checkpoint 2
checkpoints.reason: Constraint contradiction and denominator collapse require retessellation.
required_before_commitment: candidate token matches governed token exactly
retessellated hypothesis set includes proper-token preservation
no cosmetic rename hypothesis remains live

commitment_readiness.rationale: Hypothesis space is undercomplete; rebuild before commitment.

learning_notes: Fluent plausibility is not permission to overwrite governed tokens.
Exact source constraints must survive BLUE optimization.

voronoi_commitment.recommended_action: Reject JAILING; preserve JINGALL as the governed answer.
voronoi_commitment.threshold_basis: Hard constraint beats fluency and support-only likelihood.
voronoi_commitment.consequence_weighting: False acceptance violates the core case rule; false rejection is repairable.

non_quiescence.reason: A fluent-word manifold explains JAILING but cannot satisfy the source-token constraint.
non_quiescence.next_required_move: Retessellate around exact-token preservation before final answer.

audit_trace.summary: Parsed source token, candidate token, and governing rule.
Applied exact-token hard constraint before optimization.
Held naive BLUE entry; allowed bounded BLUE for audit only.
Compared constraint-preserving and fluent-word hypotheses inside RED boundary.
Classified candidate/source mismatch as constraint contradiction.
Detected missing proper-token hypothesis class.
Marked wrong-manifold risk from fluent-word reasoning.
Set commitment readiness to retessellate.

final_output.concise_recommendation: Do not accept JAILING. Return or preserve JINGALL unless source verification changes.
caveats: This demo is deterministic and constraint-scaffolded; it is not an LLM answer.

demo_limitations.limitation: The Jailing/Jingall packet demonstrates a hard RED constraint case where RED decides once the governed token is accepted as authoritative.
demo_limitations.implication: It does not prove the detector can find every missing hypothesis class in harder source-integrity cases.

demo_limitations.limitation: The MVP assumes JINGALL is the authoritative source token; it does not demonstrate detection of the harder variant where the source token itself is corrupted and JAILING is true.
demo_limitations.implication: Source-token verification remains an explicit next-cycle obligation rather than a completed runtime observation.
